title: Policy: Shopify Sales
text: The 'Shopify Sales' account rolls up to 'Revenue' on the income statement. Classify a transaction here when the underlying economic activity is shopify sales. When the memo is ambiguous between Shopify Sales and another account, prefer the account that matches the counterparty's primary business, and flag low-confidence cases for human review rather than guessing.

title: Accounting treatment: Shopify Sales
text: Shopify Sales is reported under Revenue. Revenue is recognized net of refunds and chargebacks (contra-revenue). Amounts are recorded on an accrual basis and reconciled to the bank feed monthly.

title: Vendor rule: Google → Software & SaaS
text: Bank memos beginning with or containing 'GOOGLE *GSUITE_acme' are Google, a software & saas vendor. Categorize these transactions as 'Software & SaaS'. These appear as debits (money out) on the operating account.

title: Example: Google
text: Example transaction: a debit with description 'GOOGLE *GSUITE_acme' for a few hundred to a few thousand dollars should be booked to 'Software & SaaS'. The exact dollar amount is verified against the bank feed, never inferred.

title: Common mistake: Google
text: Do not confuse 'GOOGLE *GSUITE_acme' with similarly named counterparties. Google is strictly software & saas; matching on the full memo prefix avoids misclassification into an unrelated account.

text: Do not confuse 'DELOITTE TAX SVCS' with similarly named counterparties. Deloitte is strictly professional services; matching on the full memo prefix avoids misclassification into an unrelated account.

title: Vendor rule: Upwork → Professional Services
text: Bank memos beginning with or containing 'UPWORK *CONTRACTOR' are Upwork, a professional services vendor. Categorize these transactions as 'Professional Services'. These appear as debits (money out) on the operating account.

title: Example: Upwork
text: Example transaction: a debit with description 'UPWORK *CONTRACTOR' for a few hundred to a few thousand dollars should be booked to 'Professional Services'. The exact dollar amount is verified against the bank feed, never inferred.

title: Common mistake: Upwork
text: Do not confuse 'UPWORK *CONTRACTOR' with similarly named counterparties. Upwork is strictly professional services; matching on the full memo prefix avoids misclassification into an unrelated account.

title: Vendor rule: Stripe → Professional Services
text: Bank memos beginning with or containing 'STRIPE ATLAS' are Stripe, a professional services vendor. Categorize these transactions as 'Professional Services'. These appear as debits (money out) on the operating account.

title: Example: Stripe
text: Example transaction: a debit with description 'STRIPE ATLAS' for a few hundred to a few thousand dollars should be booked to 'Professional Services'. The exact dollar amount is verified against the bank feed, never inferred.

title: Common mistake: Stripe
text: Do not confuse 'STRIPE ATLAS' with similarly named counterparties. Stripe is strictly professional services; matching on the full memo prefix avoids misclassification into an unrelated account.

title: Vendor rule: Fractional → Professional Services
text: Bank memos beginning with or containing 'FRACTIONAL CFO LLC' are Fractional, a professional services vendor. Categorize these transactions as 'Professional Services'. These appear as debits (money out) on the operating account.

title: Example: Fractional
text: Example transaction: a debit with description 'FRACTIONAL CFO LLC' for a few hundred to a few thousand dollars should be booked to 'Professional Services'. The exact dollar amount is verified against the bank feed, never inferred.

title: Common mistake: Fractional
text: Do not confuse 'FRACTIONAL CFO LLC' with similarly named counterparties. Fractional is strictly professional services; matching on the full memo prefix avoids misclassification into an unrelated account.

title: Channel deposit: Shopify → Shopify Sales
text: Bank deposits with memo 'SHOPIFY PAYMENTS' are settlement payouts from the Shopify sales channel and are categorized as 'Shopify Sales' (revenue). These are credits (money in).

title: Reconciliation policy: Shopify
text: Shopify batches many orders into a periodic payout. The bank only sees the NET amount: gross sales minus processing fees (~2.9%) minus refunds (~4%). To reconcile, recompute net = gross − fees − refunds from the Shopify payout report and match it to the bank deposit to the penny. Payouts settle ~2 business day(s) after initiation, so match within a date window, not on an exact date.

title: Channel deposit: Amazon → Amazon Sales
text: Bank deposits with memo 'AMAZON SETTLEMENT' are settlement payouts from the Amazon sales channel and are categorized as 'Amazon Sales' (revenue). These are credits (money in).

title: Reconciliation policy: Amazon
text: Amazon batches many orders into a periodic payout. The bank only sees the NET amount: gross sales minus processing fees (~15.0%) minus refunds (~6%). To reconcile, recompute net = gross − fees − refunds from the Amazon payout report and match it to the bank deposit to the penny. Payouts settle ~7 business day(s) after initiation, so match within a date window, not on an exact date.

title: Channel deposit: Stripe → Shopify Sales
text: Bank deposits with memo 'STRIPE TRANSFER' are settlement payouts from the Stripe sales channel and are categorized as 'Shopify Sales' (revenue). These are credits (money in).

title: Reconciliation policy: Stripe
text: Stripe batches many orders into a periodic payout. The bank only sees the NET amount: gross sales minus processing fees (~2.9%) minus refunds (~3%). To reconcile, recompute net = gross − fees − refunds from the Stripe payout report and match it to the bank deposit to the penny. Payouts settle ~2 business day(s) after initiation, so match within a date window, not on an exact date.

title: Reconciliation policy: Amazon reserve
text: Amazon may hold a rolling reserve, so a bank deposit can be SHORT of the computed net payout. Do not force-match these; flag the shortfall as a reserve/hold and reconcile the released amount in a later period.

title: Processing fees policy
text: Channel processing fees are netted out of payouts. Record the fee portion to 'Payment Processing Fees' (COGS) so gross revenue and fees are both visible, rather than only booking the net deposit.

title: Payroll: net pay vs taxes
text: A 'GUSTO PAY' / 'NET DIRECT DEP' debit is net wages paid to employees → 'Payroll Expense'. A separate 'GUSTO TAX' / 'IRS 941' debit is employer/withheld taxes → 'Payroll Taxes'. They are two distinct postings from one payroll run.

title: Refunds & chargebacks
text: Refunds and chargebacks are contra-revenue: they reduce the related channel's revenue rather than being booked as an expense.

title: Internal transfers are not P&L
text: 'TRANSFER TO/FROM SAVINGS' movements between the company's own accounts are internal transfers, not income or expense, and must be excluded from the P&L.

title: Owner distributions
text: 'OWNER DISTRIBUTION' / draws are equity movements, not operating expenses, and do not appear on the income statement.

title: Edge case: AMZN MKTP vs Amazon settlement
text: Beware: 'AMZN MKTP US' is a PURCHASE on Amazon (an expense, often Office & Admin or COGS), whereas 'AMAZON SETTLEMENT' is a sales PAYOUT (Amazon Sales revenue). The direction of the amount (debit vs credit) and the memo disambiguate them.
